g.data-narration: Welcome to this comprehensive lecture on Solid Waste Management Rules, 2016 - a vital topic for environmental engineering.
The SWM Rules, 2016 replaced the 2000 rules and came into effect from April 8, 2016 under the Environment Protection Act.
g.text: Replaced Municipal Solid Wastes (Management and Handling) Rules, 2000
Issued under Environment Protection Act, 1986 by Ministry of Environment

g.data-narration: Solid waste includes household, commercial, institutional waste, street sweepings, construction debris, and horticulture waste.
g.text: Note: Excludes hazardous waste and e-waste (covered under separate rules)

g.data-narration: The rules categorize waste into biodegradable, recyclable, composite, domestic hazardous, and construction-demolition waste.
Every waste generator must segregate waste into three categories using color-coded bins: green for wet, blue for dry, and red for hazardous.
g.text: ⚠️ Mandatory for all waste generators under SWM Rules 2016

g.data-narration: Waste generators must segregate waste, store in separate bins, and hand over to authorized collectors or designated collection centers.
g.text: Commercial establishments & institutions must also comply with segregation requirements

g.data-narration: Urban Local Bodies are primarily responsible for implementation: collection, transportation, processing facilities, and scientific landfill management.
ULBs must process 70% of waste by 2018 and 80% by 2020 through composting, biogas, anaerobic digestion, or other suitable technology.
g.text: ⚠️ Open burning of waste is STRICTLY PROHIBITED

g.data-narration: New landfills must be 500m from residential areas. Mandatory requirements include leachate management, gas collection, and groundwater monitoring.
g.text: Existing sites must be rehabilitated within 5 years

g.data-narration: ULBs can collect user fees based on waste quantity or type. Revenue can also be generated from compost, biogas, and recyclable sales.
g.text: Revenue Streams: Recyclable materials sale, Landfill gas utilization, tipping fees

g.data-narration: For the first time, 2016 rules explicitly cover rural areas. Village panchayats are responsible for waste management through composting or biomethanation.
Let us examine a municipality example: 50,000 households implementing segregation with color-coded bins and collection centers.
g.text: Scenario: Municipality with 50,000 households implementing SWM Rules 2016

g.data-narration: Learn to calculate composting capacity: daily waste times organic percentage times target processing, with a 1.25 design buffer.
For landfill site selection, verify minimum 500m from residential areas, flood plain location, groundwater depth, and seismic zoning.
In summary: SWM Rules 2016 mandate segregation, ULBs implement, rural areas covered, with 70-80% processing targets and 500m landfill distance.
g.text: Open Burning Prohibited • User Fees Authorized • BMW Rules Separate
